root_text.sn56.23:1.6: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisambuddhattā tathāgato ‘arahaṁ sammāsambuddho’ti vuccati.
root_text.sn56.23:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.23:1.2: “Mendicants, there are these four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.23:1.4: The noble truths of suffering, the origin of suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.23:1.5: These are the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.23:1.6: It is because he has truly understood these four noble truths that the Realized One is called ‘the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha’.
translation_text.sn56.23:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
